大伴旅人 | image = File:Otomo Tabito.jpg | caption = Ōtomo no Tabito in ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]] | birth_date = 0665 | death_date = {{death date|0731|8|31}} | nationality = {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} | occupation = {{ill|waka poet|WD=Q11122954}} | mother = {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899}} | father = {{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337}} | spouse = {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537}} | death_place= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | title= {{ill|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382}}; {{ill|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706}}; {{ill|Dazai-no-sochi|WD=Q11434484}} | children= {{ill|Q11578524|WD=Q11578524}}; {{ill|Q59134595|WD=Q59134595}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308}} | family= {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938}} | native_name= 大伴旅人 }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōtomo no Tabito'''|大伴 旅人||665 - 31 August 731}} was a Japanese [[:en:Kugyō|court noble]], military leader and poet. He is known for his military campaign against the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] and as the father of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]], who contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' alongside his father. He served as [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]] and held the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Second Rank]]. == Life == Ōtomo was born in 665, the son of [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]] [[Otomo no Otomaro]]. His mother was Iratsume, the daughter of [[Kose no Hito]]. During [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s New Year's Day celebration of 710, he led the cavalry, [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato]], [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], and others as the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the Left with his deputy, [[Hozumi no Oyu]], in a march along the [[Suzaku Avenue]].{{Cite book |last=Ujitani |first=Tsutomu |title=続日本紀 （上） |publisher=Kodansha |year=1992 |at=和銅3rd year 正月1日条, 養老4th year 6月17日条, 養老4th year 8月12日条}} In 711, he was given the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], and in 715, he was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade, [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Minister of the Center]]. In 718, he was promoted to [[:en:Chūnagon|Chūnagon]], and in 719, he was promoted Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade. In 720, when the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] erupted in [[Kyushu]] after the assassination of Governor of [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]] Yako no Maro, Ōtomo was ordered to lead Japanese forces to suppress the rebellion. After the death of [[:en:Minister of the Right|Minister of the Right]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], Ōtomo received an imperial edict to return to [[Heijō-kyō|Nara]]. However, since the Hayato had not yet been pacified, the deputy shogun and his men were ordered to continue garrisoning the area. As a result, southern Kyushu was firmly brought under the control of the Imperial court under [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In 721, he was promoted to Junior Third Rank, and in 724, he was promoted to Senior Third Rank upon the accession to the throne of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]. Around 728, Ōtomo was transferred to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] with his wife, Ōtomo no Iratsume, as Governor of Dazaifu, the second time he was sent to Kyushu after his 60th birthday, and this appointment could have been a strategic demotion by the four Fujiwara brothers, [[:en:Fujiwara no Muchimaro|Muchimaro]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fusasaki]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Umakai|Umakai]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Maro|Maro]], to oust [[:en:Prince Naga|Prince Naga]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] in power at the time.{{Cite book |last=Nomura |first=Tadao |title=律令政治の諸様相 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |year=1968 |at=神亀5th year 大伴旅人}} However, it has also been theorized that Ōtomo was transferred because of his excellent skills in diplomacy and defense in light of the international situation at the time.{{Cite book |last=Masuo |first=Shin'ichirō |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/37634692 |title=Manyō kajin to Chūgoku shisō |last2=増尾伸一郎 |date=1997 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan |isbn=4-642-02310-0 |at=大伴旅人 |oclc=37634692}} In Dazaifu, he compiled the ''Chikushi Kadan'' of the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' with [[:en:Yamanoue no Okura|Yamanoue no Okura]] and [[:en:Sami Mansei|Sami Mansei]]. After arriving in Dazaifu, his wife died.{{Cite book |title=Man'yōshū |year= |at=巻5-795}} In 730, he was appointed Dainagon.{{Cite book |last=Kuroita |first=Katsumi |title=新訂増補国史大系 Kugyō補任 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |others=国史大系編修会 |at=大伴旅人}} Tabito was a contemporary of [[:en:Hitomaro|Hitomaro]], but lacked his success in the Imperial Court. While serving as Governor-General of [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the military procuracy in northern Kyushu from 728-730, Tabito hosted a [[:en:Hanami|plum-blossom party]], encouraging the composition of poetry among his subordinates in imitation of [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese]] style elegance. He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|tanka]]'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386287]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Tabito [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese poet}} {{family name hatnote|Ōtomo|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox person | name = Ōtomo no Tabito

As a result, southern Kyushu was firmly brought under the control of the Imperial court under [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In 721, he was promoted to Junior Third Rank, and in 724, he was promoted to Senior Third Rank upon the accession to the throne of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]. Around 728, Ōtomo was transferred to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] with his wife, Ōtomo no Iratsume, as Governor of Dazaifu, the second time he was sent to Kyushu after his 60th birthday, and this appointment could have been a strategic demotion by the four Fujiwara brothers, [[:en:Fujiwara no Muchimaro|Muchimaro]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fusasaki]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Umakai|Umakai]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Maro|Maro]], to oust [[:en:Prince Naga|Prince Naga]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] in power at the time.{{Cite book |last=Nomura |first=Tadao |title=律令政治の諸様相 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |year=1968 |at=神亀5th year 大伴旅人}} However, it has also been theorized that Ōtomo was transferred because of his excellent skills in diplomacy and defense in light of the international situation at the time.{{Cite book |last=Masuo |first=Shin'ichirō |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/37634692 |title=Manyō kajin to Chūgoku shisō |last2=増尾伸一郎 |date=1997 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan |isbn=4-642-02310-0 |at=大伴旅人 |oclc=37634692}} In Dazaifu, he compiled the ''Chikushi Kadan'' of the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' with [[:en:Yamanoue no Okura|Yamanoue no Okura]] and [[:en:Sami Mansei|Sami Mansei]]. After arriving in Dazaifu, his wife died.{{Cite book |title=Man'yōshū |year= |at=巻5-795}} In 730, he was appointed Dainagon.{{Cite book |last=Kuroita |first=Katsumi |title=新訂増補国史大系 Kugyō補任 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |others=国史大系編修会 |at=大伴旅人}} Tabito was a contemporary of [[:en:Hitomaro|Hitomaro]], but lacked his success in the Imperial Court. While serving as Governor-General of [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the military procuracy in northern Kyushu from 728-730, Tabito hosted a [[:en:Hanami|plum-blossom party]], encouraging the composition of poetry among his subordinates in imitation of [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese]] style elegance. He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|tanka]]'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386287]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Tabito [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese poet}} {{family name hatnote|Ōtomo|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox person | name = Ōtomo no Tabito
大伴旅人 | image = File:Otomo Tabito.jpg | caption = Ōtomo no Tabito in ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]] | birth_date = 0665 | death_date = {{death date|0731|8|31}} | nationality = {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} | occupation = {{ill|waka poet|WD=Q11122954}} | mother = {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899}} | father = {{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337}} | spouse = {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537}} | death_place= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | title= {{ill|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382}}; {{ill|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706}}; {{ill|Dazai-no-sochi|WD=Q11434484}} | children= {{ill|Q11578524|WD=Q11578524}}; {{ill|Q59134595|WD=Q59134595}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308}} | family= {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938}} | native_name= 大伴旅人 }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōtomo no Tabito'''|大伴 旅人||665 - 31 August 731}} was a Japanese [[:en:Kugyō|court noble]], military leader and poet. He is known for his military campaign against the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] and as the father of [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]], who contributed to the compilation of the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' alongside his father. He served as [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]] and held the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Second Rank]]. == Life == Ōtomo was born in 665, the son of [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]] [[Otomo no Otomaro]]. His mother was Iratsume, the daughter of [[Kose no Hito]]. During [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]'s New Year's Day celebration of 710, he led the cavalry, [[:en:Hayato people|Hayato]], [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], and others as the [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]] of the Left with his deputy, [[Hozumi no Oyu]], in a march along the [[Suzaku Avenue]].{{Cite book |last=Ujitani |first=Tsutomu |title=続日本紀 （上） |publisher=Kodansha |year=1992 |at=和銅3rd year 正月1日条, 養老4th year 6月17日条, 養老4th year 8月12日条}} In 711, he was given the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], and in 715, he was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade, [[:en:Ministry of the Center|Minister of the Center]]. In 718, he was promoted to [[:en:Chūnagon|Chūnagon]], and in 719, he was promoted Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade. In 720, when the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] erupted in [[Kyushu]] after the assassination of Governor of [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]] Yako no Maro, Ōtomo was ordered to lead Japanese forces to suppress the rebellion. After the death of [[:en:Minister of the Right|Minister of the Right]] [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]], Ōtomo received an imperial edict to return to [[Heijō-kyō|Nara]]. However, since the Hayato had not yet been pacified, the deputy shogun and his men were ordered to continue garrisoning the area. As a result, southern Kyushu was firmly brought under the control of the Imperial court under [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In 721, he was promoted to Junior Third Rank, and in 724, he was promoted to Senior Third Rank upon the accession to the throne of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]]. Around 728, Ōtomo was transferred to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] with his wife, Ōtomo no Iratsume, as Governor of Dazaifu, the second time he was sent to Kyushu after his 60th birthday, and this appointment could have been a strategic demotion by the four Fujiwara brothers, [[:en:Fujiwara no Muchimaro|Muchimaro]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Fusasaki|Fusasaki]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Umakai|Umakai]] and [[:en:Fujiwara no Maro|Maro]], to oust [[:en:Prince Naga|Prince Naga]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] in power at the time.{{Cite book |last=Nomura |first=Tadao |title=律令政治の諸様相 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |year=1968 |at=神亀5th year 大伴旅人}} However, it has also been theorized that Ōtomo was transferred because of his excellent skills in diplomacy and defense in light of the international situation at the time.{{Cite book |last=Masuo |first=Shin'ichirō |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/37634692 |title=Manyō kajin to Chūgoku shisō |last2=増尾伸一郎 |date=1997 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan |isbn=4-642-02310-0 |at=大伴旅人 |oclc=37634692}} In Dazaifu, he compiled the ''Chikushi Kadan'' of the ''[[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]]'' with [[:en:Yamanoue no Okura|Yamanoue no Okura]] and [[:en:Sami Mansei|Sami Mansei]]. After arriving in Dazaifu, his wife died.{{Cite book |title=Man'yōshū |year= |at=巻5-795}} In 730, he was appointed Dainagon.{{Cite book |last=Kuroita |first=Katsumi |title=新訂増補国史大系 Kugyō補任 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |others=国史大系編修会 |at=大伴旅人}} Tabito was a contemporary of [[:en:Hitomaro|Hitomaro]], but lacked his success in the Imperial Court. While serving as Governor-General of [[:en:Dazaifu, Fukuoka|Dazaifu]], the military procuracy in northern Kyushu from 728-730, Tabito hosted a [[:en:Hanami|plum-blossom party]], encouraging the composition of poetry among his subordinates in imitation of [[:en:Chinese poetry|Chinese]] style elegance. He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|tanka]]'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386287]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Tabito [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese poet}} {{family name hatnote|Ōtomo|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox person | name = Ōtomo no Tabito
大伴旅人 | image = File:Otomo Tabito.jpg | caption = Ōtomo no Tabito in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' by {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai}} | birth_date = 0665 | death_date = {{death date|0731|8|31}} | nationality = {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} | occupation = {{ill|waka poet|WD=Q11122954}} | mother = {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899}} | father = {{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337}} | spouse = {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537}} | death_place= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | title= {{ill|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382}}; {{ill|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706}}; {{ill|Dazai-no-sochi|WD=Q11434484}} | children= {{ill|Q11578524|WD=Q11578524}}; {{ill|Q59134595|WD=Q59134595}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308}} | family= {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938}} | native_name= 大伴旅人 }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōtomo no Tabito'''|大伴 旅人||665 - 31 August 731}} was a Japanese {{ill|Kugyō|en|Kugyō|WD=Q3138036|lt=court noble}}, military leader and poet. He is known for his military campaign against the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] and as the father of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, who contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'' alongside his father. He served as {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and held the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Second Rank]]. == Life == Ōtomo was born in 665, the son of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} [[Otomo no Otomaro]]. His mother was Iratsume, the daughter of [[Kose no Hito]]. During {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}'s New Year's Day celebration of 710, he led the cavalry, {{ill|Hayato people|en|Hayato people|WD=Q1151764|lt=Hayato}}, {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, and others as the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} of the Left with his deputy, [[Hozumi no Oyu]], in a march along the [[Suzaku Avenue]].{{Cite book |last=Ujitani |first=Tsutomu |title=続日本紀 （上） |publisher=Kodansha |year=1992 |at=和銅3rd year 正月1日条, 養老4th year 6月17日条, 養老4th year 8月12日条}} In 711, he was given the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], and in 715, he was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade, {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Minister of the Center}}. In 718, he was promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon}}, and in 719, he was promoted Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade. In 720, when the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] erupted in [[Kyushu]] after the assassination of Governor of {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}} Yako no Maro, Ōtomo was ordered to lead Japanese forces to suppress the rebellion. After the death of {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}}, Ōtomo received an imperial edict to return to [[Heijō-kyō|Nara]]. However, since the Hayato had not yet been pacified, the deputy shogun and his men were ordered to continue garrisoning the area. As a result, southern Kyushu was firmly brought under the control of the Imperial court under {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}. In 721, he was promoted to Junior Third Rank, and in 724, he was promoted to Senior Third Rank upon the accession to the throne of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}. Around 728, Ōtomo was transferred to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} with his wife, Ōtomo no Iratsume, as Governor of Dazaifu, the second time he was sent to Kyushu after his 60th birthday, and this appointment could have been a strategic demotion by the four Fujiwara brothers, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|en|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|WD=Q2173602|lt=Fusasaki}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Umakai|en|Fujiwara no Umakai|WD=Q1370638|lt=Umakai}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Maro|en|Fujiwara no Maro|WD=Q426317|lt=Maro}}, to oust {{ill|Prince Naga|en|Prince Naga|WD=Q3403340|lt=Prince Naga}}, the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} in power at the time.{{Cite book |last=Nomura |first=Tadao |title=律令政治の諸様相 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |year=1968 |at=神亀5th year 大伴旅人}} However, it has also been theorized that Ōtomo was transferred because of his excellent skills in diplomacy and defense in light of the international situation at the time.{{Cite book |last=Masuo |first=Shin'ichirō |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/37634692 |title=Manyō kajin to Chūgoku shisō |last2=増尾伸一郎 |date=1997 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan |isbn=4-642-02310-0 |at=大伴旅人 |oclc=37634692}} In Dazaifu, he compiled the ''Chikushi Kadan'' of the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'' with {{ill|Yamanoue no Okura|en|Yamanoue no Okura|WD=Q1273642|lt=Yamanoue no Okura}} and {{ill|Sami Mansei|en|Sami Mansei|WD=Q3470785|lt=Sami Mansei}}. After arriving in Dazaifu, his wife died.{{Cite book |title=Man'yōshū |year= |at=巻5-795}} In 730, he was appointed Dainagon.{{Cite book |last=Kuroita |first=Katsumi |title=新訂増補国史大系 Kugyō補任 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |others=国史大系編修会 |at=大伴旅人}} Tabito was a contemporary of [[:en:Hitomaro|Hitomaro]], but lacked his success in the Imperial Court. While serving as Governor-General of {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}, the military procuracy in northern Kyushu from 728-730, Tabito hosted a {{ill|Hanami|en|Hanami|WD=Q210150|lt=plum-blossom party}}, encouraging the composition of poetry among his subordinates in imitation of {{ill|Chinese poetry|en|Chinese poetry|WD=Q1069928|lt=Chinese}} style elegance. He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''{{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=tanka}}'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386287]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Tabito [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese poet}} {{family name hatnote|Ōtomo|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox person | name = Ōtomo no Tabito
大伴旅人 | image = File:Otomo Tabito.jpg | caption = Ōtomo no Tabito in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' by {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai}} | birth_date = 0665 | death_date = {{death date|0731|8|31}} | nationality = {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} | occupation = {{ill|waka poet|WD=Q11122954}} | mother = {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899}} | father = {{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337}} | spouse = {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537}} | death_place= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | title= {{ill|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382}}; {{ill|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706}}; {{ill|Dazai-no-sochi|WD=Q11434484}} | children= {{ill|Q11578524|WD=Q11578524}}; {{ill|Q59134595|WD=Q59134595}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308}} | family= {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938}} | native_name= 大伴旅人 }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōtomo no Tabito'''|大伴 旅人||665 - 31 August 731}} was a Japanese {{ill|Kugyō|en|Kugyō|WD=Q3138036|lt=court noble}}, military leader and poet. He is known for his military campaign against the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] and as the father of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, who contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'' alongside his father. He served as {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and held the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Second Rank]]. == Life == Ōtomo was born in 665, the son of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} [[Otomo no Otomaro]]. His mother was Iratsume, the daughter of [[Kose no Hito]]. During {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}'s New Year's Day celebration of 710, he led the cavalry, {{ill|Hayato people|en|Hayato people|WD=Q1151764|lt=Hayato}}, {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, and others as the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} of the Left with his deputy, [[Hozumi no Oyu]], in a march along the [[Suzaku Avenue]].{{Cite book |last=Ujitani |first=Tsutomu |title=続日本紀 （上） |publisher=Kodansha |year=1992 |at=和銅3rd year 正月1日条, 養老4th year 6月17日条, 養老4th year 8月12日条}} In 711, he was given the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], and in 715, he was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade, {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Minister of the Center}}. In 718, he was promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon}}, and in 719, he was promoted Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade. In 720, when the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] erupted in [[Kyushu]] after the assassination of Governor of {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}} Yako no Maro, Ōtomo was ordered to lead Japanese forces to suppress the rebellion. After the death of {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}}, Ōtomo received an imperial edict to return to [[Heijō-kyō|Nara]]. However, since the Hayato had not yet been pacified, the deputy shogun and his men were ordered to continue garrisoning the area. As a result, southern Kyushu was firmly brought under the control of the Imperial court under {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}. In 721, he was promoted to Junior Third Rank, and in 724, he was promoted to Senior Third Rank upon the accession to the throne of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}. Around 728, Ōtomo was transferred to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} with his wife, Ōtomo no Iratsume, as Governor of Dazaifu, the second time he was sent to Kyushu after his 60th birthday, and this appointment could have been a strategic demotion by the four Fujiwara brothers, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|en|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|WD=Q2173602|lt=Fusasaki}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Umakai|en|Fujiwara no Umakai|WD=Q1370638|lt=Umakai}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Maro|en|Fujiwara no Maro|WD=Q426317|lt=Maro}}, to oust {{ill|Prince Naga|en|Prince Naga|WD=Q3403340|lt=Prince Naga}}, the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} in power at the time.{{Cite book |last=Nomura |first=Tadao |title=律令政治の諸様相 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |year=1968 |at=神亀5th year 大伴旅人}} However, it has also been theorized that Ōtomo was transferred because of his excellent skills in diplomacy and defense in light of the international situation at the time.{{Cite book |last=Masuo |first=Shin'ichirō |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/37634692 |title=Manyō kajin to Chūgoku shisō |last2=増尾伸一郎 |date=1997 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan |isbn=4-642-02310-0 |at=大伴旅人 |oclc=37634692}} In Dazaifu, he compiled the ''Chikushi Kadan'' of the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'' with {{ill|Yamanoue no Okura|en|Yamanoue no Okura|WD=Q1273642|lt=Yamanoue no Okura}} and {{ill|Sami Mansei|en|Sami Mansei|WD=Q3470785|lt=Sami Mansei}}. After arriving in Dazaifu, his wife died.{{Cite book |title=Man'yōshū |year= |at=巻5-795}} In 730, he was appointed Dainagon.{{Cite book |last=Kuroita |first=Katsumi |title=新訂増補国史大系 Kugyō補任 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |others=国史大系編修会 |at=大伴旅人}} Tabito was a contemporary of [[:en:Hitomaro|Hitomaro]], but lacked his success in the Imperial Court. While serving as Governor-General of {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}, the military procuracy in northern Kyushu from 728-730, Tabito hosted a {{ill|Hanami|en|Hanami|WD=Q210150|lt=plum-blossom party}}, encouraging the composition of poetry among his subordinates in imitation of {{ill|Chinese poetry|en|Chinese poetry|WD=Q1069928|lt=Chinese}} style elegance. He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''{{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=tanka}}'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''{{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=tanka}}'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q386287]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Tabito [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese poet}} {{family name hatnote|Ōtomo|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox person | name = Ōtomo no Tabito
大伴旅人 | image = File:Otomo Tabito.jpg | caption = Ōtomo no Tabito in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' by {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai}} | birth_date = 0665 | death_date = {{death date|0731|8|31}} | nationality = {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} | occupation = {{ill|waka poet|WD=Q11122954}} | mother = {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899}} | father = {{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337}} | spouse = {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537}} | death_place= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | title= {{ill|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382}}; {{ill|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706}}; {{ill|Dazai-no-sochi|WD=Q11434484}} | children= {{ill|Q11578524|WD=Q11578524}}; {{ill|Q59134595|WD=Q59134595}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308}} | family= {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938}} | native_name= 大伴旅人 }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōtomo no Tabito'''|大伴 旅人||665 - 31 August 731}} was a Japanese {{ill|Kugyō|en|Kugyō|WD=Q3138036|lt=court noble}}, military leader and poet. He is known for his military campaign against the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] and as the father of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, who contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'' alongside his father. He served as {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and held the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Second Rank]]. == Life == Ōtomo was born in 665, the son of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} [[Otomo no Otomaro]]. His mother was Iratsume, the daughter of [[Kose no Hito]]. During {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}'s New Year's Day celebration of 710, he led the cavalry, {{ill|Hayato people|en|Hayato people|WD=Q1151764|lt=Hayato}}, {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, and others as the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} of the Left with his deputy, [[Hozumi no Oyu]], in a march along the [[Suzaku Avenue]].{{Cite book |last=Ujitani |first=Tsutomu |title=続日本紀 （上） |publisher=Kodansha |year=1992 |at=和銅3rd year 正月1日条, 養老4th year 6月17日条, 養老4th year 8月12日条}} In 711, he was given the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], and in 715, he was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade, {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Minister of the Center}}. In 718, he was promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon}}, and in 719, he was promoted Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade. In 720, when the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] erupted in [[Kyushu]] after the assassination of Governor of {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}} Yako no Maro, Ōtomo was ordered to lead Japanese forces to suppress the rebellion. After the death of {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}}, Ōtomo received an imperial edict to return to [[Heijō-kyō|Nara]]. However, since the Hayato had not yet been pacified, the deputy shogun and his men were ordered to continue garrisoning the area. As a result, southern Kyushu was firmly brought under the control of the Imperial court under {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}. In 721, he was promoted to Junior Third Rank, and in 724, he was promoted to Senior Third Rank upon the accession to the throne of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}. Around 728, Ōtomo was transferred to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} with his wife, Ōtomo no Iratsume, as Governor of Dazaifu, the second time he was sent to Kyushu after his 60th birthday, and this appointment could have been a strategic demotion by the four Fujiwara brothers, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|en|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|WD=Q2173602|lt=Fusasaki}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Umakai|en|Fujiwara no Umakai|WD=Q1370638|lt=Umakai}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Maro|en|Fujiwara no Maro|WD=Q426317|lt=Maro}}, to oust {{ill|Prince Naga|en|Prince Naga|WD=Q3403340|lt=Prince Naga}}, the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} in power at the time.{{Cite book |last=Nomura |first=Tadao |title=律令政治の諸様相 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |year=1968 |at=神亀5th year 大伴旅人}} However, it has also been theorized that Ōtomo was transferred because of his excellent skills in diplomacy and defense in light of the international situation at the time.{{Cite book |last=Masuo |first=Shin'ichirō |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/37634692 |title=Manyō kajin to Chūgoku shisō |last2=増尾伸一郎 |date=1997 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan |isbn=4-642-02310-0 |at=大伴旅人 |oclc=37634692}} In Dazaifu, he compiled the ''Chikushi Kadan'' of the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'' with {{ill|Yamanoue no Okura|en|Yamanoue no Okura|WD=Q1273642|lt=Yamanoue no Okura}} and {{ill|Sami Mansei|en|Sami Mansei|WD=Q3470785|lt=Sami Mansei}}. After arriving in Dazaifu, his wife died.{{Cite book |title=Man'yōshū |year= |at=巻5-795}} In 730, he was appointed Dainagon.{{Cite book |last=Kuroita |first=Katsumi |title=新訂増補国史大系 Kugyō補任 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |others=国史大系編修会 |at=大伴旅人}} Tabito was a contemporary of [[:en:Hitomaro|Hitomaro]], but lacked his success in the Imperial Court. While serving as Governor-General of {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}, the military procuracy in northern Kyushu from 728-730, Tabito hosted a {{ill|Hanami|en|Hanami|WD=Q210150|lt=plum-blossom party}}, encouraging the composition of poetry among his subordinates in imitation of {{ill|Chinese poetry|en|Chinese poetry|WD=Q1069928|lt=Chinese}} style elegance. He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''{{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=tanka}}'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] {{Short description|Japanese poet}} {{family name hatnote|Ōtomo|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox person | name = Ōtomo no Tabito
大伴旅人 | image = File:Otomo Tabito.jpg | caption = Ōtomo no Tabito in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' by {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai}} | birth_date = 0665 | death_date = {{death date|0731|8|31}} | nationality = {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}} | occupation = {{ill|waka poet|WD=Q11122954}} | mother = {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899}} | father = {{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337}} | spouse = {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537}} | death_place= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | title= {{ill|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382}}; {{ill|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706}}; {{ill|Dazai-no-sochi|WD=Q11434484}} | children= {{ill|Q11578524|WD=Q11578524}}; {{ill|Q59134595|WD=Q59134595}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308}} | family= {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938}} | native_name= 大伴旅人 }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōtomo no Tabito'''|大伴 旅人||665 - 31 August 731}} was a Japanese {{ill|Kugyō|en|Kugyō|WD=Q3138036|lt=court noble}}, military leader and poet. He is known for his military campaign against the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] and as the father of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, who contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'' alongside his father. He served as {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} and held the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Second Rank]]. == Life == Ōtomo was born in 665, the son of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon}} [[Otomo no Otomaro]]. His mother was Iratsume, the daughter of [[Kose no Hito]]. During {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}'s New Year's Day celebration of 710, he led the cavalry, {{ill|Hayato people|en|Hayato people|WD=Q1151764|lt=Hayato}}, {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}, and others as the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}} of the Left with his deputy, [[Hozumi no Oyu]], in a march along the [[Suzaku Avenue]].{{Cite book |last=Ujitani |first=Tsutomu |title=続日本紀 （上） |publisher=Kodansha |year=1992 |at=和銅3rd year 正月1日条, 養老4th year 6月17日条, 養老4th year 8月12日条}} In 711, he was given the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], and in 715, he was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade, {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Minister of the Center}}. In 718, he was promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon}}, and in 719, he was promoted Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade. In 720, when the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] erupted in [[Kyushu]] after the assassination of Governor of {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}} Yako no Maro, Ōtomo was ordered to lead Japanese forces to suppress the rebellion. After the death of {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}}, Ōtomo received an imperial edict to return to [[Heijō-kyō|Nara]]. However, since the Hayato had not yet been pacified, the deputy shogun and his men were ordered to continue garrisoning the area. As a result, southern Kyushu was firmly brought under the control of the Imperial court under {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei}}. In 721, he was promoted to Junior Third Rank, and in 724, he was promoted to Senior Third Rank upon the accession to the throne of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}}. Around 728, Ōtomo was transferred to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} with his wife, Ōtomo no Iratsume, as Governor of Dazaifu, the second time he was sent to Kyushu after his 60th birthday, and this appointment could have been a strategic demotion by the four Fujiwara brothers, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|en|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|WD=Q2173602|lt=Fusasaki}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Umakai|en|Fujiwara no Umakai|WD=Q1370638|lt=Umakai}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Maro|en|Fujiwara no Maro|WD=Q426317|lt=Maro}}, to oust {{ill|Prince Naga|en|Prince Naga|WD=Q3403340|lt=Prince Naga}}, the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} in power at the time.{{Cite book |last=Nomura |first=Tadao |title=律令政治の諸様相 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |year=1968 |at=神亀5th year 大伴旅人}} However, it has also been theorized that Ōtomo was transferred because of his excellent skills in diplomacy and defense in light of the international situation at the time.{{Cite book |last=Masuo |first=Shin'ichirō |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/37634692 |title=Manyō kajin to Chūgoku shisō |last2=増尾伸一郎 |date=1997 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan |isbn=4-642-02310-0 |at=大伴旅人 |oclc=37634692}} In Dazaifu, he compiled the ''Chikushi Kadan'' of the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū}}'' with {{ill|Yamanoue no Okura|en|Yamanoue no Okura|WD=Q1273642|lt=Yamanoue no Okura}} and {{ill|Sami Mansei|en|Sami Mansei|WD=Q3470785|lt=Sami Mansei}}. After arriving in Dazaifu, his wife died.{{Cite book |title=Man'yōshū |year= |at=巻5-795}} In 730, he was appointed Dainagon.{{Cite book |last=Kuroita |first=Katsumi |title=新訂増補国史大系 Kugyō補任 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |others=国史大系編修会 |at=大伴旅人}} Tabito was a contemporary of [[:en:Hitomaro|Hitomaro]], but lacked his success in the Imperial Court. While serving as Governor-General of {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu}}, the military procuracy in northern Kyushu from 728-730, Tabito hosted a {{ill|Hanami|en|Hanami|WD=Q210150|lt=plum-blossom party}}, encouraging the composition of poetry among his subordinates in imitation of {{ill|Chinese poetry|en|Chinese poetry|WD=Q1069928|lt=Chinese}} style elegance. He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''{{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=tanka}}'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

While serving as Governor-General of {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu, Fukuoka}}, the military procuracy in northern Kyushu from 728-730, Tabito hosted a {{ill|Hanami|en|Hanami|WD=Q210150|lt=plum-blossom party|1=Hanami}}, encouraging the composition of poetry among his subordinates in imitation of {{ill|Chinese poetry|en|Chinese poetry|WD=Q1069928|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese poetry}} style elegance. He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''{{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=tanka|1=Waka (poetry)}}'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

[[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]][[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]][[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]][[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]][[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]][[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]][[ja:大伴旅人]][[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]][[ru:Отомо-но Табито]][[uk:Отомо но Табіто]][[zh:大伴旅人]] {{Short description|Japanese poet}} {{family name hatnote|Ōtomo|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox person | name = Ōtomo no Tabito
大伴旅人 | image = File:Otomo Tabito.jpg | caption = Ōtomo no Tabito in ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' by {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai|1=Kikuchi Yōsai}} | birth_date = 0665 | death_date = {{death date|0731|8|31}} | nationality = {{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} | occupation = {{ill|waka poet|WD=Q11122954|1=Waka poet}} | mother = {{ill|Q97378899|WD=Q97378899|1=Kose no Iratsume}} | father = {{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337|1=Otomo no Otomaro}} | spouse = {{ill|Q11368537|WD=Q11368537|1=Tambi no Iratsume}} | death_place= {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | title= {{ill|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|1=Chūnagon}}; {{ill|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|1=Dainagon}}; {{ill|Dazai-no-sochi|WD=Q11434484|1=Dazai no Sochi}} | children= {{ill|Q11578524|WD=Q11578524|1=Tome no Nyōrō}}; {{ill|Q59134595|WD=Q59134595|1=Otomo no Fudemochi}}; {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}} | family= {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} | native_name= 大伴旅人 }} {{Nihongo|'''Ōtomo no Tabito'''|大伴 旅人||665 - 31 August 731}} was a Japanese {{ill|Kugyō|en|Kugyō|WD=Q3138036|lt=court noble|1=Kugyō}}, military leader and poet. He is known for his military campaign against the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] and as the father of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}, who contributed to the compilation of the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}'' alongside his father. He served as {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}} and held the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Second Rank]]. == Life == Ōtomo was born in 665, the son of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}} [[Otomo no Otomaro]]. His mother was Iratsume, the daughter of [[Kose no Hito]]. During {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}'s New Year's Day celebration of 710, he led the cavalry, {{ill|Hayato people|en|Hayato people|WD=Q1151764|lt=Hayato|1=Hayato people}}, {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, and others as the {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}} of the Left with his deputy, [[Hozumi no Oyu]], in a march along the [[Suzaku Avenue]].{{Cite book |last=Ujitani |first=Tsutomu |title=続日本紀 （上） |publisher=Kodansha |year=1992 |at=和銅3rd year 正月1日条, 養老4th year 6月17日条, 養老4th year 8月12日条}} In 711, he was given the [[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|court Rank]] of [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], and in 715, he was promoted to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade, {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Minister of the Center|1=Ministry of the Center}}. In 718, he was promoted to {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}}, and in 719, he was promoted Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade. In 720, when the [[:en:Hayato Rebellion|Hayato Rebellion]] erupted in [[Kyushu]] after the assassination of Governor of {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province|1=Ōsumi Province}} Yako no Maro, Ōtomo was ordered to lead Japanese forces to suppress the rebellion. After the death of {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Minister of the Right|1=Minister of the Right}} {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}}, Ōtomo received an imperial edict to return to [[Heijō-kyō|Nara]]. However, since the Hayato had not yet been pacified, the deputy shogun and his men were ordered to continue garrisoning the area. As a result, southern Kyushu was firmly brought under the control of the Imperial court under {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}. In 721, he was promoted to Junior Third Rank, and in 724, he was promoted to Senior Third Rank upon the accession to the throne of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}. Around 728, Ōtomo was transferred to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}} with his wife, Ōtomo no Iratsume, as Governor of Dazaifu, the second time he was sent to Kyushu after his 60th birthday, and this appointment could have been a strategic demotion by the four Fujiwara brothers, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Muchimaro|1=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|en|Fujiwara no Fusasaki|WD=Q2173602|lt=Fusasaki|1=Fujiwara no Fusasaki}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Umakai|en|Fujiwara no Umakai|WD=Q1370638|lt=Umakai|1=Fujiwara no Umakai}} and {{ill|Fujiwara no Maro|en|Fujiwara no Maro|WD=Q426317|lt=Maro|1=Fujiwara no Maro}}, to oust {{ill|Prince Naga|en|Prince Naga|WD=Q3403340|lt=Prince Naga|1=Prince Naga}}, the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} in power at the time.{{Cite book |last=Nomura |first=Tadao |title=律令政治の諸様相 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |year=1968 |at=神亀5th year 大伴旅人}} However, it has also been theorized that Ōtomo was transferred because of his excellent skills in diplomacy and defense in light of the international situation at the time.{{Cite book |last=Masuo |first=Shin'ichirō |url=https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/37634692 |title=Manyō kajin to Chūgoku shisō |last2=増尾伸一郎 |date=1997 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan |isbn=4-642-02310-0 |at=大伴旅人 |oclc=37634692}} In Dazaifu, he compiled the ''Chikushi Kadan'' of the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}'' with {{ill|Yamanoue no Okura|en|Yamanoue no Okura|WD=Q1273642|lt=Yamanoue no Okura|1=Yamanoue no Okura}} and {{ill|Sami Mansei|en|Sami Mansei|WD=Q3470785|lt=Sami Mansei|1=Sami Mansei}}. After arriving in Dazaifu, his wife died.{{Cite book |title=Man'yōshū |year= |at=巻5-795}} In 730, he was appointed Dainagon.{{Cite book |last=Kuroita |first=Katsumi |title=新訂増補国史大系 Kugyō補任 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |others=国史大系編修会 |at=大伴旅人}} Tabito was a contemporary of [[:en:Hitomaro|Hitomaro]], but lacked his success in the Imperial Court. While serving as Governor-General of {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu, Fukuoka}}, the military procuracy in northern Kyushu from 728-730, Tabito hosted a {{ill|Hanami|en|Hanami|WD=Q210150|lt=plum-blossom party|1=Hanami}}, encouraging the composition of poetry among his subordinates in imitation of {{ill|Chinese poetry|en|Chinese poetry|WD=Q1069928|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese poetry}} style elegance. He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''{{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=tanka|1=Waka (poetry)}}'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

After arriving in Dazaifu, his wife died.{{Cite book |title=Man'yōshū |year= |at=巻5-795}} In 730, he was appointed Dainagon.{{Cite book |last=Kuroita |first=Katsumi |title=新訂増補国史大系 Kugyō補任 |publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan |others=国史大系編修会 |at=大伴旅人}} Tabito was a contemporary of [[:en:Hitomaro|Hitomaro]], but lacked his success in the Imperial Court. While serving as Governor-General of {{ill|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|en|Dazaifu, Fukuoka|WD=Q728218|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu, Fukuoka}}, the military procuracy in northern Kyushu from 728-730, Tabito hosted a {{ill|Hanami|en|Hanami|WD=Q210150|lt=plum-blossom party|1=Hanami}}, encouraging the composition of poetry among his subordinates in imitation of {{ill|Chinese poetry|en|Chinese poetry|WD=Q1069928|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese poetry}} style elegance. He also showed his Chinese education in his set of thirteen ''{{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=tanka|1=Waka (poetry)}}'' in praise of [[sake]]. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{Japan-writer-stub}} [[en:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[az:Otomo no Tabito]] [[ca:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[de:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[eo:Otomo-no Tabito]] [[es:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[fa:اوتومو نو تابیتو]] [[fr:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[hi:ओतोमो ताबितो]] [[hu:Ótomo no Tabito]] [[hy:Օտոմո Տաբիտո]] [[ja:大伴旅人]] [[pl:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pms:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[pt:Ōtomo no Tabito]] [[ru:Отомо-но Табито]] [[uk:Отомо но Табіто]] [[zh:大伴旅人]] {{wikidata link|Q386287}} [[Category:660s births]] [[Category:731 deaths]] [[Category:Date of birth unknown]] [[Category:Date of death unknown]] [[Category:Japanese poets]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Place of birth unknown]] [[Category:Place of death unknown]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
